lessons.title: ICD Basics
summary: ICD-10 is a statistical system used to classify diseases and related health problems.
Its main job is to turn written diagnoses into standard codes for recording, reporting, and analysis.
key_points: ICD-10 is a statistical system used to classify diseases and related health problems.
Its main job is to turn written diagnoses into standard codes for recording, reporting, and analysis.
It is used for mortality and morbidity data, public health tracking, and health information comparison across places...
It can code diseases, injuries, symptoms, abnormal findings, complaints, and some service-contact situations.
lessons.example: A written diagnosis like cholera can be converted into an ICD code.
lessons.common_trap: Thinking ICD is only for confirmed diseases.
lessons.quick_tip: Remember the big idea: ICD standardizes health information.

lessons.title: Code Structure
summary: The first character is a letter, and the next characters are numbers.
key_points: ICD-10 uses an alphanumeric format.
The first character is a letter, and the next characters are numbers.
The basic category is a 3-character code, and many categories expand to a 4-character subcategory after a decimal point.
ICD-10 is organized in a hierarchy: chapter, block, category, and subcategory.
lessons.common_trap: Mixing up a chapter with a block.
lessons.quick_tip: For exams, memorize the ladder: chapter -> block -> 3-character category -> 4-character subcategory.

lessons.title: How To Assign A Code
summary: Correct coding requires both the Alphabetical Index and the Tabular List.
Start by identifying the type of statement being coded, such as disease, injury, symptom, or external cause.
key_points: Correct coding requires both the Alphabetical Index and the Tabular List.
Start by identifying the type of statement being coded, such as disease, injury, symptom, or external cause.
Look up the lead term in the Alphabetical Index first.
Read any modifiers, notes, and cross-references such as 'see' and 'see also'.
lessons.example: For fracture of neck of femur, first find the lead term in the Index, then confirm the final code in the...
lessons.common_trap: Choosing a code from the Alphabetical Index and stopping there.
lessons.quick_tip: Use this order in exams: Index first, Tabular List second, notes last.

lessons.title: Coding Rules & Special Logic
summary: Some conditions need more than one code because ICD captures both the cause and the clinical problem.
In the dagger-asterisk system, the dagger code identifies the underlying disease and the asterisk code identifies the...
key_points: Some conditions need more than one code because ICD captures both the cause and the clinical problem.
In the dagger-asterisk system, the dagger code identifies the underlying disease and the asterisk code identifies the...
The dagger code is the primary code and the asterisk code must never be used alone.
ICD also allows optional additional coding in some situations, such as external causes, infecting organisms, and...
lessons.example: Measles pneumonia may require both a dagger code for measles with pneumonia and an asterisk code for the...
lessons.common_trap: Using an asterisk code by itself.
lessons.quick_tip: Think 'cause plus manifestation' for dagger-asterisk questions.

lessons.title: Guidelines for Morbidity Coding
summary: For morbidity, the main condition is the condition mainly responsible for the episode of care.
If more than one condition qualifies, choose the one that used the greatest resources.
key_points: For morbidity, the main condition is the condition mainly responsible for the episode of care.
If more than one condition qualifies, choose the one that used the greatest resources.
Other conditions should also be recorded if they affected management during the same episode.
If no definite diagnosis is established, code the best available symptom, abnormal finding, or problem.
lessons.example: If a patient is treated mainly for acute cholecystitis, that is the main condition.
lessons.common_trap: Choosing the first diagnosis listed instead of the condition chiefly responsible for care.
lessons.quick_tip: For morbidity questions, ask: what was mainly treated or investigated?

lessons.title: Important Exam Concepts
summary: ICD is a statistical classification, so categories must be mutually exclusive and broad enough to cover all reportable...
Because no classification can list every detail separately, ICD uses residual categories such as 'other' and...
key_points: ICD is a statistical classification, so categories must be mutually exclusive and broad enough to cover all reportable...
Because no classification can list every detail separately, ICD uses residual categories such as 'other' and...
Volume 1 contains the Tabular List and the main classification.
Volume 2 gives instructions and coding guidance.
lessons.example: If a code title says bones and joints, it may include bones, joints, or both because 'and' means 'and/or'.
lessons.quick_tip: Memorize the three volumes: Volume 1 = Tabular List, Volume 2 = guidance, Volume 3 = Index.
